name: Comparer.Equals
signature: Comparer.Equals( comparer as function, x as any, y as any ) as logical
description: Returns a `logical` value based on the equality check over the two given values, `x` and `y`, using the provided `comparer`. `comparer` is a `Comparer` which is used to control the comparison. A comparer is a function that accepts two arguments and returns -1, 0, or 1 based on whether the first value is less than, equal to, or greater than the second. Comparers can be used to provide case-insensitive or culture and locale-aware comparisons. The following built-in comparers are available in the formula language: * [`Comparer.Ordinal`](comparer-ordinal.md): Used to perform an exact ordinal comparison * [`Comparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase`](comparer-ordinalignorecase.md): Used to perform an exact ordinal case-insensitive comparison * [`Comparer.FromCulture`](comparer-fromculture.md): Used to perform a culture-aware comparison

name: Comparer.FromCulture
signature: Comparer.FromCulture(culture as text, optional ignoreCase as nullable logical) as function
description: Returns a comparer function that uses the `culture` and the case-sensitivity specified by `ignoreCase` to perform comparisons. A comparer function accepts two arguments and returns -1, 0, or 1 based on whether the first value is less than, equal to, or greater than the second. The default value for `ignoreCase` is false. The `culture` should be one of the locales supported by the .NET framework (for example, "en-US").

name: Comparer.Ordinal
signature: Comparer.Ordinal(x as any, y as any) as number
description: Returns a comparer function which uses Ordinal rules to compare the provided values `x` and `y`. A comparer function accepts two arguments and returns -1, 0, or 1 based on whether the first value is less than, equal to, or greater than the second.

name: Comparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase
signature: Comparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase(x as any, y as any) as number
description: Returns a case-insensitive comparer function which uses Ordinal rules to compare the provided values `x` and `y`. A comparer function accepts two arguments and returns -1, 0, or 1 based on whether the first value is less than, equal to, or greater than the second.
